title: FG normal-form indices
summary: Introduces the three index directions and the coordinate triple used in Fefferman–Graham normal-form component calculations.

mathematicalContent.heading: Index convention
mathematicalContent.body: In chosen-scale normal coordinates (t,x,ρ), the index 0 denotes the scale direction t, a base index i denotes a tangent direction on M, and ∞ denotes the transverse coordinate ρ. The base index type remains abstract so it may later represent a coordinate frame or any finite formal index set.

mathematicalContent.heading: What is formalized
mathematicalContent.body: The file defines only the index type, its Kronecker delta, and a coordinate data record. It does not construct a coordinate chart, tangent basis, differential form, or smooth atlas.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An ambient normal-form index is either the scale index 0, a base-manifold index i, or the transverse index ∞.
correspondence.leanType: inductive AmbientIndex (ι : Type u) : Type u

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This is the Kronecker delta on the complete ambient index set.
correspondence.leanType: def kronecker [DecidableEq ι] (I J : AmbientIndex ι) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Every diagonal entry of the ambient Kronecker delta equals one.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_self [DecidableEq ι] (I : AmbientIndex ι) : kronecker I I = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The scale and transverse directions are distinct.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_zero_inf [DecidableEq ι] : kronecker (ι := ι) zero inf = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The reversed transverse–scale Kronecker component also vanishes.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_inf_zero [DecidableEq ι] : kronecker (ι := ι) inf zero = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The scale index is distinct from every base index.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_zero_base [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) : kronecker zero (base i) = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The reversed base–scale Kronecker component vanishes.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_base_zero [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) : kronecker (base i) zero = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A formal normal-coordinate point stores a real scale coordinate t, a base point x ∈ M, and a real transverse coordinate ρ.

references.note: Source for the chosen-scale coordinates (t,x,ρ) and the 0, base, and ∞ index convention.

limitations: This module is an index and coordinate scaffold. NormalCoord is a data record, not a smooth coordinate chart, and no tensor calculus is constructed here.
